purpose: Pure-JS unified diff renderer for the currently selected file. Reads gitDiff and gitSelectedFile from AppContext (or accepts diff/filePath/fileStatus props for embedded use). Internally parses raw `git diff` output via parseDiff() — extracts hunks, classifies each line as added/removed/context/info ('\ No newline at end of file'), and tracks old/new line numbers from the @@ -X,Y +A,B @@ hunk headers. Detects binary diffs by sniffing the first 300 chars for 'Binary files', 'GIT binary patch', or 'Binary file ' tokens. Renders a sticky file header with status badge, additions/deletions counts, then each hunk with its header line and per-line numbers + content. Empty/null diff and binary diff render empty-state placeholders.

exports.name: DiffViewer
exports.signature: { diff?: string, filePath?: string, fileStatus?: string }

exports.name: parseDiff
exports.signature: (raw: string) => { hunks: [], additions: number, deletions: number }

gotchas: parseDiff skips header lines aggressively (diff --git, index, ---/+++, new/deleted file, mode changes, similarity, rename/copy) — if the backend emits any non-standard header it will be silently dropped.
Binary detection sniffs the first 300 chars only — large binary diffs with leading text headers may not be detected.
Lines starting with '\\' are treated as informational ('\\ No newline at end of file') and rendered italic+dim — do not treat them as content.
Line numbering relies on the hunk header regex: `/^@@\s+-(\d+)(?:,\d+)?\s+\+(\d+)(?:,\d+)?\s+@@(.*)?$/` — non-standard hunk headers (e.g. combined-diff '@@@') are not handled.

The component supports two invocation modes: prop-driven (diff/filePath/fileStatus) AND context-driven (gitDiff/gitSelectedFile). Props take precedence; missing props fall through to context — be explicit when embedding to avoid surprise.
parseDiff is also exported as a named export for reuse — any change to its return shape is a breaking change for external consumers.
patterns: Two-column line numbers (oldNum / newNum) + content with prefix + / − / space — classic GitHub-style unified diff.
useMemo for both binary detection and parsing — diff text can be large, recomputing on every render is wasteful.
STATUS_CLASS map (M/A/D/R/U/?/T/C) reused independently of ChangesPanel's STATUS_MAP — kept here so DiffViewer is decoupled.
conventions: Empty states use git-manager-empty class; line classes are git-manager-diff-line + added|removed|context.
additions/deletions counters render only when > 0 to keep the header compact.
